message:
fix(raptorq): canonicalize duplicate SparseRow entries, type-aware schema validation, multi-block decode threshold

SparseRow::new now handles unsorted and duplicate-index entries:
duplicate indices are merged via GF(256) addition (XOR), and entries
that cancel to zero are removed. Both SparseRow::new and
SparseRow::singleton now panic on out-of-range indices instead of
silently constructing invalid rows. This fixes incorrect matrix
operations when constructing sparse rows from unordered symbol data.

ObjectParams::min_symbols_for_decode now returns total_source_symbols()
(symbols_per_block × num_source_blocks) instead of just
symbols_per_block. For multi-block objects this was causing premature
decode attempts with insufficient symbols, leading to
InsufficientSymbols errors that were confusing because the collector
reported enough symbols per its (incorrect) threshold.

validate_unit_log_json now performs type-aware validation of
decode_stats fields: unsigned integer fields (k, loss_pct, dropped,
etc.) are validated as u64, string fields (fallback_reason,
hard_regime_branch, conservative_fallback_reason) are validated as
strings, and hard_regime_activated is validated as boolean. The seed
field also gets explicit type checking. Previously all fields used a
simple null-or-missing check that accepted any JSON type.

Tests added for duplicate-index canonicalization, out-of-range panics,
multi-block insufficient-symbols rejection, and multi-block
symbols_needed calculation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
